response.justification: Wei Chen brings over a decade of solid software engineering experience with TypeScript/Node.js and current exposure to agentic workflows and MCP infrastructure at re:cinq, which is directly relevant to the role. However, the candidate lacks Python — the dominant language in AI/ML engineering — and the resume contains no explicit mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or any named AI framework (LangGraph, LlamaIndex, etc.). The AI-specific credentials are almost entirely confined to the most recent role (2025–present), falling well short of the 8-year AI delivery requirement. The broad generalist background is valuable but does not substitute for deep, demonstrated GenAI production experience.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq includes MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure — directly aligned with the job's key technologies.
Demonstrated Staff-level seniority across multiple companies with cross-domain technical breadth (cloud, data pipelines, embedded, mobile).
AWS CDK experience at Optilyz and high-throughput data integration work at Dunnhumby show real cloud and pipeline credibility.
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills list; AI/ML production work overwhelmingly relies on Python, and this gap is a significant disqualifier for an 8-year AI-solutions requirement.
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation/observability frameworks, or any named multi-agent framework (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.).
Meaningful AI engineering experience appears limited to the current role starting April 2025 — far below the minimum 8 years of AI-driven solution delivery specified.

key_factors.factor: No RAG or vector DB experience
